source: After English class, I review my notes for ten minutes without making a big deal of it, since the deadline quietly shifted.
When my teacher emails back, I finish the missing homework section as a quick reset, while my phone stays on silent.
Before homeroom, I rewrite three lines of my essay without making a big deal of it, so I am not scrambling at night.
Right after school, I make a small checklist for tomorrow just to be safe, and I can actually sleep on time.
When the bell rings, I reread the last chapter quickly as a quick reset, so I am not scrambling at night.
Since our project got moved, I ask my sister to quiz me just in case, while my phone stays on silent.
If we finish early, I reread the last chapter quickly just to be safe, so I am not scrambling at night.
After math class, I fix the code example in our report just in case, without skipping dinner.
Once I grab a snack, I fix the code example in our report as a quick reset, while the hallway is still calm.
When my group finally replies, I ask my friend to check my outline as a quick reset, even if my bus is crowded.
